strategy.name: High-Timeframe BTC Analysis (Vays + Brandt + DonAlt)
sources: Tone Vays: Weekly RSI + MACD + WMA 50/200 — momentum reversal on weekly closes
Peter Brandt: SMA 8/18 crossover + classical chart pattern breakouts with volume
DonAlt: Horizontal S/R zones + Swing Failure Patterns — range edges only
primary_timeframes: Weekly (macro bias — all three traders agree this is primary)

indicators.rsi_14_weekly: Tone Vays: Bullish below 30 with volume spike + bullish divergence. Bearish above 70 with MACD divergence. Only act on weekly candle CLOSES.
indicators.macd_12_26_9: Tone Vays: Trend direction and divergence confirmation on weekly chart. Used alongside RSI — don't act on MACD alone.
indicators.wma_50: Tone Vays: Intermediate trend filter on weekly chart.
indicators.wma_200: Tone Vays: Macro bull/bear dividing line. Reclaim after dip = strong buy signal.
indicators.sma_8: Peter Brandt: Short-term trend proxy. When 8 SMA > 18 SMA = uptrend.
indicators.sma_18: Peter Brandt: Medium-term trend proxy. When 8 SMA < 18 SMA = downtrend. Cross = trend change signal.
indicators.volume: Peter Brandt: Confirms pattern breakouts. High volume breakout = valid. Low volume = suspect.

bullish: Weekly RSI < 30 with volume spike (Vays capitulation buy)
Bullish RSI divergence on weekly (price lower low, RSI higher low)
Price above WMA 200 on weekly chart (macro bull)
8 SMA crossed above 18 SMA on daily (Brandt trend proxy)
Price near key weekly/monthly support zone (DonAlt range low)
Swing Failure Pattern: wick below support, closes back above (DonAlt SFP)
bearish: Weekly RSI > 70 with bearish MACD divergence (Vays exhaustion)
Price lost WMA 200 on weekly close (macro bear signal)
8 SMA crossed below 18 SMA on daily (Brandt downtrend)
Price near key weekly/monthly resistance zone (DonAlt range high)
Swing Failure Pattern: wick above resistance, closes back below (DonAlt SFP)
neutral: Price in the middle of a S/R range (DonAlt: only trade edges)
No confirmed weekly candle close signal (Vays: never trade intrabar)

long: Identify key weekly/monthly support zone first (DonAlt)
Wait for weekly candle to close — never enter intrabar (Vays)
Confirm: 8 SMA > 18 SMA on daily OR RSI < 30 weekly with divergence
Optional: SFP confirmation (wick below support, daily close above)
short: Identify key weekly/monthly resistance zone first (DonAlt)
Wait for weekly candle close with RSI > 70 + bearish MACD divergence (Vays)
Confirm: 8 SMA < 18 SMA on daily (Brandt trend proxy)
Optional: SFP at resistance (wick above, daily close below)

exit_rules: Vays: Exit when weekly RSI reaches opposite extreme (sold into RSI > 70 if bought at < 30)
Brandt: Exit when chart pattern target is reached OR pattern is invalidated
DonAlt: Exit near opposite edge of S/R range. If support breaks on weekly close → cut immediately.
Universal: Invalidation = weekly close beyond stop level. No exceptions.
risk_rules: Always define invalidation point BEFORE entering (all three traders agree)
Position size based on distance to stop-loss (Brandt: risk-defined entries only)
If WMA 200 weekly lost → reduce size significantly or stay out
Patience: wait for setup to come to you, never chase (DonAlt principle)
Most consolidations last 30-60+ days before resolution — don't force trades (DonAlt)
what_they_agree_on: Weekly chart closes are the most important signal — lower timeframes are noise
Fewer indicators = less noise. Quality setups over quantity.
Risk-defined entries: always know your stop before entering
Patience: let the setup come to you
notes: Strategy synthesized from three of the most credible and transparent public BTC traders. Tone Vays (ex-Wall Street quant, Bitcoin maximalist), Peter Brandt (50+ years professional trader, classical chartist), DonAlt (Swiss trader, price structure specialist). All three focus on high timeframes (weekly/daily) and strongly discourage day trading. This is a swing/position trading framework, NOT a scalping strategy.
